title: SSC CPO 2026 — Sub Inspector Exam
meta_description: SSC CPO 2026: Sub Inspector in Delhi Police, CAPF (BSF, CRPF, CISF). Notification May 31. 4,000+ vacancies. Physical test critical.

hero.one_liner: Sub Inspector recruitment in Delhi Police, Central Armed Police Forces (CAPF), and CISF. Officer-rank entry with ₹35–50K salary.

what_is_it: SSC CPO (Central Police Organisation) exam conducted annually by Staff Selection Commission recruits Sub Inspectors (SI) in Delhi Police and Central Armed Police Forces (CAPF) including BSF, CRPF, CISF, ITBP, and SSB. SI is officer-rank entry — you directly join as a uniformed commissioned officer. Delhi Police SI become Station House Officers (SHO) or investigation officers in India's capital. CAPF SI posts involve commanding units of 50–100 paramilitary personnel in critical locations (border security, counter-terrorism, VIP protection). With 4,000+ vacancies, SSC CPO is one of India's largest competitive exams.

Selection process has 4 stages: (1) Paper 1 (CBT) — General Intelligence, GK, Quantitative Aptitude, English (200 MCQs in 120 min). (2) Physical Standard Test (PST) — Height, chest measurement, running speed (qualifying). (3) Paper 2 (English descriptive) — Qualifying, only for those clearing PST. (4) Medical examination (qualifying). Unlike civil services which interview 2x candidates, SSC CPO is purely objective — no interview. This makes it more predictable but demands higher precision.

Starting salary at Level 6 (₹35,400–1,12,400 basic) means ₹40,000–50,000/month in-hand depending on allowances and location. Delhi Police SI has advantages: posting in national capital, law & order work (investigation, crime prevention), professional development through police academy training. CAPF SI involves paramilitary discipline, border/counter-terror operations, challenging environments but greater adventure and higher allowances in difficult postings. Both pathways lead to officer-class career in one of India's most powerful forces.

key_details.value: Height 170 cm, Chest 80/85 cm, 100m run 16 sec, Long jump 3.65m

key_details.label: Physical (Female)
key_details.value: Height 157 cm, 100m run 18 sec, Long jump 2.70m

exam_pattern.description: 200 MCQs in 4 sections: GI (50), GK (50), QA (50), English (50). Each MCQ worth 1 mark. Negative marking: 0.25 mark per wrong.
Height, chest, running, jumping. Must pass to appear in Paper 2.
Essay writing, letter/formal writing. Only marks don't matter; must clear (typically 40+ out of 100). Tests communication skills.
Fitness for police/paramilitary service. Medical examination, vision check, hearing test.

Better — home posting, weekends off possible

Adventure seekers, physically fit, patriotic motivation, unmarried preferred

extra_sections.heading: SSC CPO Selection Process & Physical Fitness Requirements
content: Stage 1: Paper 1 (CBT) — Merit-Determining. 200 MCQs (50 each: GI, GK, QA, English) in 120 minutes. Negative marking: -0.25 per wrong.
Cutoff: 115–130/200 typical (58–65%). This stage is merit-determining — your Paper 1 score decides your final ranking.
Unlike civil services (where interview has 250 marks), in CPO the written exam is everything.
Stage 2: Physical Standard Test (PST) — Qualifying, Strict Cutoffs. Male candidates: Height 170 cm (no relaxation, rigid), Chest 80/85 cm.
Female: Height 157 cm. Running: 100m in 16 seconds (male) / 18 seconds (female) — this is the toughest part.
Long jump: 3.65m (male) / 2.70m (female). Many candidates clear Paper 1 but fail PST due to inadequate physical fitness.
There's NO interview to compensate for lower physical performance.
Stage 3: Paper 2 (English) — Qualifying Only. Essay writing, letter writing.
Candidates need 40+/100 to pass. Marks don't affect final ranking.
This stage tests written English communication — essential for police/paramilitary work (filing FIRs, writing reports, testimonies in court).
Stage 4: Medical Examination — Qualifying. Vision test (Snellen chart, minimum vision 6/6 correctable), hearing test, general health.
Any disqualifying condition (flat feet, color-blindness, psychological issues) ends candidature.

preparation_tips: SSC CPO Paper 1 is identical to SSC CGL Tier 1 in difficulty and syllabus. If preparing for CGL, use CPO as 2nd exam with zero additional written-exam preparation. Both exams are conducted separately but same written test difficulty.

Physical fitness is equally important as written exam. Start running practice now: (1) Sprint training (100m daily, aiming 16 seconds for men, 18 for women). (2) Long jump practice (approach speed + technique). (3) Strength training (push-ups, pull-ups for endurance). (4) Flexibility (stretching to prevent injury). Many candidates clear Paper 1 but fail Physical Test due to lack of fitness — don't let this be you.
English Paper 2 is qualifying — you need only 40+/100. Don't stress over it. Focus 80% on Paper 1, 15% on fitness, 5% on English Mains. Paper 1 is merit-determining.

Time management in Paper 1: 200 Qs in 120 min = 36 sec per question. Speed is critical. Strategy: (1) Reasoning (50 Qs) in 45 min (complex, needs thought). (2) GK (50 Qs) in 30 min (straightforward). (3) QA (50 Qs) in 35 min (calculations). (4) English (50 Qs) in 10 min (comprehension usually easier). Practice mocks at this pace.

faqs.question: Age 20–25 is very strict — can I appeal for relaxation?
faqs.answer: SSC doesn't give age relaxation for CPO. Age limit is firm. If you're 26 years old, you cannot apply. This exam is designed for younger candidates (post-graduation at 22–23, apply at 23–24). Plan accordingly. Don't delay graduation or exam prep.

faqs.question: If I clear Paper 1 but fail Physical Test, can I re-appear?
faqs.answer: No. Physical Test is qualifying — if you fail (e.g., don't meet height requirement, fail 100m run timing), your candidature ends. You cannot re-attempt in same exam cycle. You must apply again next year and clear from Paper 1. This is why physical preparation must start 6 months before exam.

faqs.question: Delhi Police SI vs CGL Group B posts — which is better?
faqs.answer: Delhi Police SI: Officer rank, commanding authority, ₹40,000–55K/month. CGL Group B: Lower rank (UDC/LDC), clerical role, ₹25,000–35K/month. Police SI is clearly better in rank, authority, and pay. But police work is demanding (12-hour shifts, law & order complexity). CGL is better for stability and work-life balance. Choose based on career aspirations.

faqs.question: Is there interview in SSC CPO?
faqs.answer: No. SSC CPO is purely objective-based selection. Paper 1 (merit-determining) + Paper 2 (qualifying) + Physical & Medical (qualifying). No interview. This makes SSC CPO fully predictable unlike civil services.

syllabus.subject: General Intelligence & Reasoning
syllabus.topics: Analogy, classification, series, coding-decoding, matrix, word arrangement, Venn diagram, direction, blood relations, syllogism, statement-conclusion, non-verbal reasoning

syllabus.subject: General Knowledge & Awareness
syllabus.topics: Current affairs (last 6 months), Indian history, geography, polity, economics, general science, static GK, sports, awards

syllabus.subject: Quantitative Aptitude
syllabus.topics: Number system, percentage, ratio, profit & loss, SI/CI, time & work, time & distance, algebra, geometry, trigonometry, mensuration, data interpretation

syllabus.subject: English Comprehension
syllabus.topics: Reading comprehension, error spotting, fill in blanks, synonym/antonym, one word substitution, idioms, sentence improvement, active-passive, direct-indirect

study_plan.tasks: SSC CPO syllabus overlaps 90% with CGL. If preparing for CGL, you're automatically prepared for CPO. Focus on all 4 sections equally. Physical fitness training alongside — CPO has PET.
2 mock tests per week. Practice 1600m run in 5 minutes (male), 1 km in 5:40 (female). Long jump, high jump practice daily. Current affairs monthly compilations.
Daily mocks. Revise formula sheets. GK current affairs of last 6 months. Target: 350+ out of 400 in Paper 1 for SI/ASI selection.
